statement: NVIDIA's B200 delivers about 4.5 FP8 dense TFLOPS per watt (4,500 TFLOPS at a 1,000W TDP); AMD's MI350X posts comparable raw FP8 throughput (~4,600 TFLOPS) but across a wider 750-1,400W power envelope, and the older MI300X sits at ~3.5 TFLOPS/W.
why: FP8 dense TFLOPS divided by rated TDP is the headline efficiency spec operators use to size clusters against a fixed power budget.

impact.mechanism: Higher delivered FLOPS per watt lets operators pack more usable compute into fixed datacenter power, easing effective compute supply per megawatt.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)

confidence.basis: Secondary analyst spec compilation; perf/W is a direct ratio of two stated specs.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: value 2.25 -> 4.5 (tol 1%); sources: GPUAdvisor — NVIDIA B300 Ultra vs AMD MI355X (March 2026), Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
